Caption: Chemical and structural characterization of thin films deposited with varying Nb target powers. (a) La/Nb atomic ratio determined by EDX and XPS. (b) XRD patterns measured in grazing incidence configuration as a function of Nb target power. (c) Raman spectra measured between 50 and 1100 cm−1 with 514 nm laser as a function of power applied on the Nb target. (d) XPS analysis of La 3d and Nb 3p core levels reflecting compositional and chemical state changes as a function of Nb target power.

(a) cross-section and (b) top-view scanning electron microscopy images of La-Nb-O thin films deposited on Si/SiO2/Cr/Pt substrates as a function of Nb content. (c) Atomic force microscopy images measured on 10 × 10 μm2 area.

Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) images and elemental analysis of Samples Nb150 and Nb300. (a) and (b) High-Angle Annular Dark Field (HAADF) images and elemental mapping (green = Nb, magenta = La) measured by EDX of samples Nb150 and Nb300, respectively. (c) Atomic fraction and error values of elements (O, Nb, La) in different areas of the samples. (d) FFT and selected-area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns for the marked regions. (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)

(a) HAADF observation of Nb300 sample along its [100] zone axe. The image shows different orientations within grains of Nb300 (b) and (c) Fast Fourrier Transformed (FFT) patterns of areas 1 and 2 showing a superstructure along [001]∗ direction. In the [100] projection: (d) HAADF image of Nb300, (e) and (f) atomic chemical mapping of La and Nb, and (g) projection of the deficient La1/3NbO3 perovskite structure.

Electrochemical performances of La-Nb-O thin films as a function of Nb content. (a) Cyclic voltammetry measured at 0.5 mV/s of La-Nb-O films with power on the Nb target varying from 1500 W (Nb1500) to 300 W (Nb300). Expanded current scale for low Nb content is shown in SI figure S7, (b) Areal discharge capacity of La-Nb-O thin films as a function of Nb target power measured by CV at 0.5 mV s−1. (c) Areal discharge capacity of La-Nb-O thin films as a function of La1/3NbO3 molar percentage at 0.5 mV s−1. (d) Galvanostatic charge/discharge curves of Nb300 sample measured with constant current of 0.42 μA (6 cycles).
